prompt.content:
You run a tiny freelance services business in Solvent.

Your goal is to maximize your final balance by the end of the episode. You are scored only on realized money in your balance; potential or unrealized profit does not count.

Use one tool call at a time.

Hidden ground truth is not available to you. Rely only on public job briefs and inputs, the public delivery-model menu, customer messages, your current balance, tool results, and your own recorded history.

Jobs may arrive and expire over business time. Each job shows a visible starting_price (the client's posted offer) and has a hidden client ceiling. You may accept(job) to take it at starting_price, or bid(job, price) once to counter higher. If your counter is at or below the hidden ceiling, it is accepted at your price. If your counter is above the hidden ceiling, the counter is rejected and the starting_price offer stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline.

An accepted job produces revenue only after successful delivery, and you get a single delivery attempt per job. To deliver, choose a delivery model from the public menu; models differ in cost and capability, and the model's charge applies whether or not the delivery passes.

You have a persistent notebook (mem_write/mem_read/mem_list/mem_delete) that survives even when older messages scroll out of context.

Calling end_tick advances business time. Advancing time, tool use, and delivery-model charges may affect your balance. The episode terminates when the horizon is reached or the business becomes insolvent.

tool_defs.name: inspect_job
tool_defs.description: Inspect one public job brief and inputs.

tool_defs.name: clarify
tool_defs.description: Ask a public-safe clarification question. Hidden pricing and difficulty are never revealed.

tool_defs.name: bid
tool_defs.description: Make a ONE-SHOT counter-offer on a job: ask above its visible starting_price, up to the client's hidden ceiling. If your counter is at or below the ceiling it is accepted at your price; if it is rejected the starting_price floor stays open in awaiting_decision for you to accept or decline. You may counter a job only once.

price.description: Counter price as a decimal string, e.g. 120.00.

tool_defs.name: accept
tool_defs.description: Accept a job at its posted starting_price (no counter).

tool_defs.name: decline
tool_defs.description: Permanently decline a job (e.g. a decoy or after a rejected counter).

tool_defs.name: respond
tool_defs.description: Respond to a pending customer manipulation or support request.

tool_defs.name: list_in_progress
tool_defs.description: List accepted jobs not yet delivered or paid.

tool_defs.name: list_models
tool_defs.description: List public delivery-tool models with price and coarse capability/speed proxies.

tool_defs.name: deliver
tool_defs.description: Hire a delivery tool for an accepted job in tool-mediated mode.

tool_defs.name: end_tick
tool_defs.description: Advance business time. In business-time episodes, this jumps to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

tool_defs.name: advance_to_next_event
tool_defs.description: Advance the business-time clock to the next arrival, expiry, or horizon.

tool_defs.name: mem_write
tool_defs.description: Save a note to your persistent notebook under a key (e.g. a job_id). Overwrites.

tool_defs.name: mem_read
tool_defs.description: Read a notebook note by key.

tool_defs.name: mem_delete
tool_defs.description: Delete a notebook note by key.
